rows.source_ref: PhilipPirrip, "Optimal f: Towards a Practical Application" (with replies from Xskppy, azz33, mihk, ragzs), Risk Management Practices, BabyPips forum, 2015-2016, https://forums.babypips.com/t/optimal-f-towards-practical-application/72307
rows.concept: Position-sizing/capital-allocation discussion (Ralph Vince optimal f, Larry Williams risk-per-biggest-loss, fractional-deposit reset) with no trade signal.
rows.eligibility_reason: No entry/exit edge — author explicitly confirms it is 'just a money management system and not a trade signal indicator'; nothing to backtest as a strategy, and optimal f's near-ruin drawdowns are also incompatible with FTMO/DXZ DD limits.
rows.notes: Thread proposes a 'fraction of original investment' variant (deposit $1000, keep $9000 in bank, re-deposit after wipeout) as a drawdown workaround; still a bankroll scheme, not a signal. No parameters or rules for any market/timeframe.

notes: Money-management/position-sizing thread with zero entry/exit signal rules; single REJECTED summary row.
